title: senml: Record derives Copy but contains references
description:
File: rust/lichen-senml/src/record.rs
Line: 20

The Record struct derives Copy:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq)]
pub struct Record<'a> {
```

While this is technically valid because all fields are either Option<&str> or Option<f64>/Option<bool> (all Copy), it has subtle implications:

1. Copy semantics mean users might not realize they're working with shared references
2. Modifying the copied Record's references still points to the original data
3. This is confusing API design - a borrowed type that implements Copy

The struct design is correct for zero-copy parsing, but Copy is unusual for a type containing references. Clone alone would be more idiomatic.

Impact: API confusion; developers might expect Copy to create independent data.

Recommendation: Consider removing Copy derive, keeping only Clone. Document that the struct borrows from input data.
